parameters.content:
## 📰 News Scraper — RSS → (Translate) → Publish

Fetches articles from your RSS feeds, dedupes them, enriches them with an
image + full text from the article page, optionally **translates them into
every language you configure**, and POSTs the result to your own API.

### Quick start
1. Open **⚙️ Config** → set your API endpoint + auth (and `languages`,
   default `['en']` = English only).
2. Open **📰 Prepare Sources** → add your feeds & category IDs.
3. *Only if you use more than one language or foreign-language feeds:*
   select your **OpenAI credential** on the *Translate* node.
4. Hit **Execute workflow** to test, then enable the *Schedule* trigger.

📖 Full docs: see `README.md` and `docs/` in the repository.

## ✏️ EDIT THESE TWO NODES

**⚙️ Config** — API endpoint, auth header, **languages** (default
`['en']`; add codes like `['en','es']` to publish multilingual),
item limits, placeholder image.

**📰 Prepare Sources** — your RSS feeds and the slug → category-ID map
for your backend. Everything else works without changes.

## 🔁 Per-source loop

*Per Source* processes one feed at a time (isolates failures: one broken
feed never kills the run). *Fetch RSS* and most downstream nodes use
**continue-on-error**, so the loop always completes.

*Build Articles* takes the newest N items, skips items already posted
(dedupe via workflow static data), generates one field set per configured
language, and emits a `_skip` marker when a feed yields nothing —
*Has Articles?* routes those straight back to the loop.

## 🖼️ Enrich + 🌐 Translate (optional)

*Fetch Article Page* downloads the article HTML (never errors, 8s timeout).
*Resolve Image* picks the best image (RSS → og:image → twitter:image) and
extracts the full article text when it beats the feed summary.

*Needs Translation?* checks if any configured language is still missing —
if not (e.g. default English-only with English feeds), **the LLM is
skipped entirely** and no OpenAI key is needed. Otherwise *Translate*
fills ONLY the empty language fields; filled fields pass through
unchanged. If the LLM fails, the article still publishes via fallbacks.

## 🚀 Publish

*Build Payload* assembles the final object with one field set per
configured language (guaranteed non-empty). *POST Article* sends it to
`Config.apiUrl` with your auth header. *Mark Seen* records the article
as published **only on a 2xx response** — failures are retried on the
next run.

Payload contract → `docs/configuration.md`.

nodes.name: Run
nodes.notes: Manual trigger for testing. For production, enable the Schedule trigger below instead.

nodes.name: Schedule
nodes.notes: Optional scheduled run (default: every 6 hours). Enable this node and activate the workflow for production. IMPORTANT: the dedupe store (workflow static data) only persists for active/scheduled runs, not manual test runs.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: ⚙️ EDIT ME — central configuration: API endpoint, auth header, LANGUAGES (default ['en']), per-feed item limit, text length caps, placeholder image. Every other node reads from here.

nodes.name: Prepare Sources
nodes.notes: 📰 EDIT ME — define your RSS feeds (SOURCES) and the slug → numeric category-ID map for your backend (CATEGORIES). Ships with 5 public example feeds.

nodes.name: Per Source
nodes.notes: Loops over sources one at a time so a single broken feed can't break the whole run. Output 1 = 'all done', Output 2 = next source.

nodes.name: Done
nodes.notes: Final summary item once every source has been processed.

nodes.name: Fetch RSS
nodes.notes: Reads the current source's feed. customFields exposes enclosure/media tags so Build Articles can pick a feed image. Continues on error so dead feeds don't stop the run.

nodes.name: Build Articles
nodes.notes: Normalizes RSS items into article objects: newest N items, dedupe against previously-posted IDs, one field set per configured language (feed's own language pre-filled, others left empty for Translate).

nodes.name: Has Articles?
nodes.notes: TRUE branch = feed produced nothing new (skip marker) → loop to next source. FALSE branch = real articles → enrich & publish.

nodes.name: Skip Source
nodes.notes: Records WHY a source was skipped (no_rss_items / all_already_seen / no_usable_url) and hands control back to the loop.

nodes.name: Fetch Article Page
nodes.notes: Downloads the article's HTML page (8s timeout, never errors) so we can extract an og:image and the full article text. If it fails, the feed summary is used instead.

nodes.name: Resolve Image
nodes.notes: Picks the best image (RSS image → og:image → twitter:image) and upgrades the description to the full page text when it's longer than the feed summary.

nodes.name: Needs Translation?
nodes.notes: TRUE = at least one configured language is still missing text → run the LLM. FALSE = everything already filled (e.g. default English-only with English feeds) → skip Translate entirely; no OpenAI key needed.

values.content:
You localize news articles into multiple languages.
You receive:
1. A JSON object of article fields. Each key ends with a language code suffix (e.g. title_en, description_es, short_description_ar). Some fields are filled, some are empty strings.
2. The original source text of the article (title, short_description, description) and its language.

Rules:
- For every EMPTY field, produce it by translating the corresponding source text (title -> title_<lang>, short_description -> short_description_<lang>, description -> description_<lang>) into the language indicated by the field's suffix.
- For every NON-EMPTY field, return it UNCHANGED. Do not retranslate, rewrite, summarise, or "improve" filled fields.
- Never leave a field empty.
- Preserve named entities (people, places, brands). Do not add commentary.
- Respond with ONLY a JSON object containing exactly the same keys as the input field object, all values as strings. No markdown, no extra keys, no explanations.

nodes.name: Translate
nodes.notes: 🔑 SETUP ONLY IF you configure multiple languages or foreign-language feeds — attach your own OpenAI credential here. Fills ONLY the empty language fields; never rewrites filled ones. Keys are dynamic (one set per language), so output is prompt-enforced JSON parsed defensively in Build Payload. Swap the model if you prefer another. Continues on error: Build Payload has fallbacks.

nodes.name: Build Payload
nodes.notes: Merges the translation (if any) into the article and builds the final API payload with one field set per configured language. Defensive: guarantees every text field is non-empty even if the LLM failed or was skipped.

nodes.name: POST Article
nodes.notes: Sends the article to YOUR endpoint — URL, auth header and body (incl. per-language fields) all come from Config / Build Payload, nothing is hardcoded here. fullResponse + neverError let Mark Seen inspect the status code.

nodes.name: Mark Seen
nodes.notes: Marks the article as published in the dedupe store only on a 2xx response, then loops back for the next source. Failed posts are retried next run.
